tools.name: schedule_create
tools.description: Create a recurring scheduled automation that sends a message at a cron or RRULE interval. ONLY use this when the user explicitly wants something to run on a schedule (e.g. "every day at 9am", "weekly on Mondays", "every hour"). Do NOT use this for "add to my tasks" or "add to my queue" — use task_list_add for those requests instead.

name.description: A human-readable name for the scheduled task

syntax.description: Schedule syntax type. Auto-detected if omitted.

timezone.description: IANA timezone (e.g. "America/Los_Angeles"). Defaults to system timezone if omitted.

message.description: The message to send to the assistant when the schedule triggers

enabled.description: Whether the job is enabled immediately. Defaults to true.

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: schedule_list
tools.description: List recurring scheduled automations (cron jobs or RRULE schedules), or show details and recent runs for a specific one. For the user's task queue, use task_list_show instead.

enabled_only.description: When true, only show enabled jobs. Defaults to false.

job_id.description: If provided, show detailed info and recent runs for this specific job.

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: schedule_update
tools.description: Update an existing recurring scheduled automation (expression, syntax, message, name, or enabled state)

job_id.description: The ID of the schedule to update

syntax.description: Schedule syntax type. Auto-detected if omitted.

message.description: New message to send when triggered

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: schedule_delete
tools.description: Delete a recurring scheduled automation and all its run history

job_id.description: The ID of the schedule to delete

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called
